dlna.desc1: Once the DLNA function is enabled, devices that support DLNA (such as TV, computer and TV box) can access files on NAS devices on a LAN. DLNA supports only file data such as videos, music, and pictures. By default, the content is classified into folders such as videos, music, and photos.
dlna.desc2: Note: Access to NAS device via DLNA is anonymous by default. When the DLNA accesses too many files, it takes time to load the file list.
dlna.desc3: Once the DLNA service is enabled, shared multimedia files can display on your computer or TV through LAN.
dlna.desc5: Adding or removing media file paths will restart DLNA service.
dlna.removeContent: Removing the path will affect devices that are playing through DLNA. Are you sure you wish to remove it?
dlna.removeErrContent: Remove all invalid paths from the list?
dlna.emptyPath: You Did Not Select Any Path
dlna.footerTips: To add a media file path, you need to restart the dlna service, which may affect devices that are using the DLNA service
dlna.notSupported: Root directory selection is currently not supported, please choose another subdirectory.
dlna.remondContent: After enabling the DLNA service, the added [media resources] will be anonymously accessible to other devices on the local network. For your privacy and security, please be cautious when adding personal folders or sensitive resources.
dlna.remondTips: Don't show this reminder next time
